rulings.Case Title: **Transferred to Contra Costa County** MARIANA GREUB ET AL VS. AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO COMPEL ATTENDANCE AT DEPOSITION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, February 11, 2026, Line 2, PLAINTIFF MARIANA GREUB, ESTON GREUB's MOTION TO COMPEL ATTENDANCE AT DEPOSITION. 3 - The motion to compel American Honda Motor Co., Inc.'s attendance at deposition is denied without prejudice to Plaintiff's service of a new PMQ deposition notice concerning authentication of business records after she has received the responses to the form interrogatory that the court has ordered today, and after she has met and conferred with American Honda's counsel about the revised responses. The form interrogatory resopnses that the court has ordered substantially duplicate the noticed deposition topics. Once Plaintiff receives substantive responses to the form interrogatory, the necessary topics for this PMQ may be substantially narrowed. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA).

rulings.Case Title: **Transferred to Contra Costa County** MARIANA GREUB ET AL VS. AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatory 17.1, Set Two, From Defendant American Honda Motor Co., Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, February 11, 2026, Line 2, PLAINTIFF MARIANA GREUB, ESTON GREUB's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatory 17.1, Set Two, From Defendant American Honda Motor Co., Inc. 1 - The motion to compel further responses to form interrogatory 17.1, set two, is granted. Plaintiff seeks discovery concerning documents she intends to use at trial; her discovery is targeted at preliminary facts that establish whether a business records hearsay exception is present. This is a permissible use of discovery. Defendant objects on grounds of relevance and contends that the Cadena litigation documents may not be used at trial. The court concludes that Plaintiff has made a threshold showing of relevance sufficient to satisfy Code of Civil Procedure, 2017.010. Defendant has not substantiated its burdens objections. (See West Pico Furniture Co. of Los Angeles v. Superior Court (1961) 56 Cal.2d 407, 417 ["The objection based upon burden must be sustained by evidence showing the quantum of work required, while to support an objection of oppression there must be some showing either of an intent to create an unreasonable burden or that the ultimate effect of the burden is incommensurate with the result sought."].) Defendant shall provide further responses by March 4, 2026. No sanctions are awarded. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. rulings.Case Title: DONALD NORMAN VS. PATRICK STRATEMAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, February 11, 2026, Line 3, CROSS DEFENDANT DONALD NORMAN's DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT. (Complete tentative ruling emailed to the parties). 2 - Hearing required concerning the form of order regarding the civil harassment cause of action. The demurrer is otherwise overruled. The issue under Code of Civil Procedure, 430.10, subdivision (e), is, taking the facts properly pleaded and properly noticed as true, whether the challenged cause of action necessarily fails to state a claim for relief. (Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) In assessing whether the complaint states a cause of action, the court accepts all properly pleaded material facts, but not contentions, deductions, or conclusions of fact or law. (Minton v. Dignity Health (2019) 39 Cal.App.5th 1155, 1161.) The court liberally construes the complaint. (Code Civ. Proc., 452.) The request for judicial notice is granted. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. rulings.Case Title: KUN WANG VS. UBER TECHNOLOGY INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Evidentiary And Issue Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Attorney
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, February 11, 2026, Line 4, DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'s (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS UBER TECHNOLOGY, INC.) Motion For Evidentiary And Issue Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Attorney. Defendant's motion for an evidentiary or issue sanction is granted in part and denied in part. Defendant seeks "an order precluding Plaintiff from offering evidence or argument that Uber controls the behavior of drivers who use the Uber app." (Notice of Motion, 2:14-15.) The court denies the evidentiary sanction but finds a misuse of discovery and orders plaintiff's counsel to pay $950 in monetary sanctions to defendant. The court has discretion to find a misuse of the discovery process where counsel's conduct presents undue opportunities for coaching, even if there is no direct evidence of coaching. (See Agnone v. Agnone (2025) 111 Cal.App.5th 758, 766-767.) The court finds misuse here. Plaintiff's counsel sought a break over objection while a question was pending, and plaintiff's responses after the break are suggestive. However, defendant's proposed issue sanction reaches too far. "Discovery sanctions should be appropriate to the dereliction, and should not exceed that which is required to protect the interests of the party entitled to but denied discovery." (Doppes v. Bentley Motors, Inc. (2009) 174 Cal.App. 4th 967, 992 (internal quotations omitted).) Defendant's sanction would likely put defendant in a better position than if the misuse had not occurred. The courts a lesser monetary sanction. Plaintiff's counsel is ordered to pay $950 in sanctions to defendant by March 11, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-25-623697 WITH CASE CGC-25-621499
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, February 11, 2026, Line 8, DEFENDANT CENTURYLINK COMMUNICATIONS, LLC's, MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-25-623697 WITH CASE CGC-25-621499. 4 - Hearing required. The court is inclined to grant consolidation but is considering continuing the hearing on this motion for a few weeks to enable the parties to meet and confer about the discovery issues raised in Inflect's opposition. The court will be prepared at the hearing to discuss those issues with the parties as well. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA).

rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDER XUE VS. G680 GROUP, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Order Sustaining Defendants Demurrer
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, February 11, 2026, Line 12, PLAINTIFF ALEXANDER XUE's Motion For Relief From Order Sustaining Defendants Demurrer. The motion for relief from order sustaining defendant's demurrer is granted. The order sustaining the demurrer is set aside and the demurrer is set for hearing on March 10, 2026. Plaintiff may file a timely opposition brief pursuant to the Code and defendant may reply. Code of Civil Procedure, 473, subdivision (b), allows the court to relieve a party from an "order . . . taken against the party through the party's mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect." Plaintiff Alexander Xue demonstrates that he was not served with the moving papers on demurrer in compliance with the Code and attests that he was unaware of the demurrer until the day before the hearing. That meet and confer conversations had occurred, or that Xue communicates with defendant's counsel by email, does not relieve defendant from the responsibility of making Code-compliant service. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA).
